info.title: TatvaCare CRM API
info.description:
Reference for integrating with the TatvaCare CRM — the single source of truth for patient leads across every TatvaCare product line.

## What this API does

Write leads (create / update / upsert / delete, singly or in bulk). Read leads. Discover at runtime exactly the fields you may send. Attach files. It's the systems door into the CRM — external systems put leads in and read state back, while internal teams work those leads in the CRM web app.

## The shape

```
 CRM Lead (one lead per patient × product line + group)
   ├── Plan Profile           — what plan / PSP / insurance / drug program / subscription
   ├── Lab Profile            — lab values, vitals, conditions, scores
   └── Care & Providers       — coaches, RM, therapist, doctor, hospital
```

The lead carries identity, contact, and routing fields. The three children carry concern-specific data. A patient typically has 1–3 child rows populated. Multi-program is normal.

**Lead grain:** a lead is unique per `mobile_no` + `custom_vertical` (product line) + `custom_group`. The same phone number on a *different* product line or group is a **separate** lead — one patient can own several leads, one per line they're enrolled in. Re-sending the same (phone + line + group) trio updates that one lead; it never duplicates.

## Where to start

- **New here?** Read [Welcome](/docs/welcome), then [Authentication](/docs/api/authentication), then the [Quickstart](/docs/quickstart).
- **New to Frappe?** Start with [What's a DocType?](/docs/frappe-doctypes) — the concepts this API is built on, in plain English.
- **Already have a key?** Jump to [Create a lead](/docs/api/leads).
- **Need to know what fields you may send?** Call `lead_schema` — it returns exactly the fields YOUR key may send/read (see [Schema](/docs/api/schema)).

Every endpoint is tested live in the **API Reference** — open an endpoint, paste your key, and hit **Send**.

tags.name: Partner Lead API
tags.description:
## The partner lead API

One set of gated methods under `/api/method/tatva_connect.api.partner.<fn>` is the **entire surface** for partners — raw `/api/resource/CRM Lead` returns **403** for partner keys. Internal System Manager keys may still use raw REST, but these methods are the supported, idempotent path for everyone.

A **partner** key has routing **forced** from its `Lead API Mapping` row, is **scoped** to its own line, and may only use its **Allowed Fields** subset. A **trusted internal** key sends routing in the body, sees all lines, and uses the full catalog. Both call the *same* endpoints — the difference is config on the key, not different code.

Call `lead_schema` first to discover exactly what YOUR key may send/read.

tags.name: Authentication
tags.description:
## How to authenticate

Every request needs an `Authorization` header. Format:

```
Authorization: token <api_key>:<api_secret>
```

The word `token` is **literal lowercase**, followed by a single space, then `api_key`, a colon `:`, then `api_secret`. No `Bearer`, no quotes, no JWT.

Example (placeholder — not a real key):

```
Authorization: token 000000000000000:000000000000000
```

### Two kinds of key

There are exactly two caller types, and which one you are decides how you authenticate and what you can do:

| Caller | Who | Routing | Field access | Scope |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| **Trusted internal** | First-party systems (e.g. MyTatvaCore). Key user has the **System Manager** role. | You **send** routing (`custom_vertical` / `custom_group` / `custom_current_program` / `source`) in the body. | Full catalog. | All leads. |
| **External partner** | Third parties (insurers, referral partners). Key user is **role-less** + has one `Lead API Mapping` row. | Routing is **forced server-side** from your mapping row — any routing you send is ignored. | Only the fields ticked in your mapping's **Allowed Fields** grid (empty grid = full catalog). | Only leads on YOUR line (your vertical + group). |

Both call the **same** partner endpoints (`/api/method/tatva_connect.api.partner.*`). The difference is configuration on your key, not different code.

### How to get a key

1. **Email** `pareekshith.bompally@tatvacare.in` with: integration name, who owns it, expected request volume, and which fields you need to send/read.
2. **You receive** a dedicated user (e.g. `mytatva-writer@tatvacare.in`) plus its `api_key:api_secret` pair.
   - **Internal** integrations get the **System Manager** role (so they may send routing and use the full field catalog).
   - **External partners** get a **role-less** user plus one **`Lead API Mapping`** row that pins their forced routing (`source` / `vertical` / `group` / `program`) and their **Allowed Fields** subset. Your `source` value is created for you. You cannot move a lead to another line, see other lines' leads, see other doctypes, or send/read any field outside your grid.
3. **Store** the pair as one environment variable: `FRAPPE_API_TOKEN="000000000000000:000000000000000"`. Treat it like a database password.

### Discover what you can do — call `lead_schema`

Don't hardcode a field list. `GET /api/method/tatva_connect.api.partner.lead_schema` returns exactly the parent + child fields YOUR key may send/read (with type/required/options) and your routing mode (`forced` for partners, `caller-supplied` for internal). Two partners calling it get different lists. This — plus a one-page note of your forced line — is your contract.

### One key per integration

Don't share keys across systems. Each integration gets its own user + key so we can revoke / rotate / audit per integration. If you need a key for staging plus a separate one for prod, request both.

### Verify your key works

The fastest handshake — returns the authenticated user's email if the key is valid:

```bash
curl -H 'Authorization: token <api_key>:<api_secret>' \
     https://one.tatvacare.in/api/method/frappe.auth.get_logged_user
```

Success returns `{"message":"mytatva-writer@tatvacare.in"}`. A `401` means the header is malformed (most often: missing the literal word `token`, extra spaces, swapped key/secret order).

### Rotation, revocation, leakage

If a key is ever leaked, exposed in logs, committed to git — email CRM Ops immediately. We'll revoke it (the user gets disabled) and reissue a fresh pair. The new pair has different values; update your env var and redeploy.

Routine rotation isn't enforced today; we'll add a schedule if/when compliance requires it.

### What the key can NOT do

- It can't bypass field-level validation (Link values must exist, required fields can't be empty).
- It can't call non-whitelisted methods (anything not documented here).
- It can't see leads outside the CRM site (it's scoped to this site only).
- **Partner keys** additionally can't touch raw `/api/resource/*` (you get **403**) — the partner methods under `/api/method/tatva_connect.api.partner.*` are your entire surface — and can't set routing, see another line's leads, or use any field outside their Allowed Fields grid.

tags.name: Leads
tags.description:
## The partner lead API

Leads are written and read through **one set of gated methods** under `/api/method/tatva_connect.api.partner.<fn>`. This is the entire surface for partners — raw `/api/resource/CRM Lead` is **not** available to partner keys (it returns 403). Internal System Manager keys may still use raw REST, but these methods are the supported, idempotent path for everyone.

| Verb | Method | Does |
|---|---|---|
| GET | `lead_schema` | Discovery — exactly the fields YOUR key may send/read + your routing mode. |
| GET | `lead_get` | Read one lead by `name` or `mobile_no` (scoped to your line). |
| POST | `lead_create` | Create-or-upsert by phone. Returns the CRM `name`. |
| PUT | `lead_update` | Update a lead by CRM `name` (the id `lead_create` returned). |
| DELETE | `lead_delete` | Delete a lead by CRM `name` (your line only; a lead with linked activity can't be deleted). |
| POST | `lead_create_bulk` | `{"leads":[{...}, …]}` (≤ 100). Partial success. |
| PUT | `lead_update_bulk` | `{"updates":[{"name":…, …}, …]}` (≤ 100). Partial success. |
| DELETE | `lead_delete_bulk` | `{"names":[…]}` (≤ 100). Partial success. |
| POST | `lead_get_bulk` | `{"names":[…]}` **or** `{"mobile_nos":[…]}` (≤ 100). Out-of-scope ids omitted. |
| GET | `lead_list` | Curated filters + pagination, always scoped to your line. |

**`lead_create` is the default write path for ~99% of integrations.** It's idempotent on the lead grain (`mobile_no` + your line + group), safe to retry, and handles the lookup-or-create dance for you.

### Lead grain & dedup

A lead is unique per `mobile_no` + `custom_vertical` (product line) + `custom_group`. `lead_create` finds the lead matching that trio and updates it, or creates a new one. The same phone on a *different* line/group is a separate lead. Partners never send routing — their line is forced from their mapping — so for them dedup is simply phone within their fixed line.

### Profile child write semantics (important)

Profile children (`custom_plan_profile`, `custom_lab_profile`) are sent as **embedded arrays** inside the body. There's at most ONE row per profile per lead today. Writes **merge field-by-field** into the existing row: fields you send are overlaid, fields you omit are left alone — a partial write never wipes the row. (This is true for all of `lead_create` / `lead_update` and their bulk forms.)

### Routing is not yours to set (partners)

The routing fields `custom_vertical` + `custom_group` are `permlevel=1` (manager-only). `custom_current_program` is now **`permlevel=0`** (reps may self-handoff a program within their line). Partners never set any of these — routing (`source` / `vertical` / `group` / `program`) is **forced server-side** from your `Lead API Mapping` row, and any routing you send is silently ignored. Trusted internal (System Manager) keys send routing in the body.

### Field validation summary

- **Allowed fields are per-key.** Call `lead_schema` to see exactly what your key may send. Anything outside your Allowed Fields grid is dropped (partners) — `mobile_no` is always allowed (the anchor).
- **Required to create**: `mobile_no` (the anchor). `first_name` and `status` are default-filled if missing (`(no name)` and `New`).
- **Required IF status is Junk or Unqualified**: `lost_reason` (Frappe raises `ValidationError` otherwise).
- **Link-validated** (values must exist in their master): `status`, `source`, `custom_vertical`, `custom_group`, `custom_current_program`, `substage` on Plan Profile (format `{program}-{substage_name}`).
- **Never write**: `name` (autonamed), `lead_name` (computed), `creation`, `modified`. Routing + `lead_owner` are set by the platform, not partners.

---

## How to read leads from the CRM

Three paths: `lead_get` (one, by `name` or `mobile_no`), `lead_get_bulk` (many, by `names` or `mobile_nos`), `lead_list` (filtered + paginated). All return only your allowed fields and only leads on your line.

### `lead_list` filters & pagination

`lead_list` honours a **curated, safe set of filters only** — never arbitrary fields:

| Parameter | Meaning |
|---|---|
| `status` | exact lead status |
| `created_after` / `created_before` | `creation` date range |
| `updated_after` / `updated_before` | `modified` date range |
| `mobile_no` | exact phone |
| `limit` | page size (default 20, max 200) |
| `offset` | rows to skip (paging) |

Results are always auto-scoped to your line and return **curated parent fields, no children** — use `lead_get` for the full record. Response: `{ok, total, count, offset, limit, leads:[…]}`.

```bash
curl -G -H 'Authorization: token <api_key>:<api_secret>' \
     --data-urlencode 'status=New' \
     --data-urlencode 'updated_after=2026-05-01' \
     --data-urlencode 'limit=50' \
     https://one.tatvacare.in/api/method/tatva_connect.api.partner.lead_list
```

### Bulk partial-success shape

Every bulk method runs each record in its own savepoint — one bad row reports its error, the rest still commit. Response: `{ok, total, succeeded, failed, results:[{index, ok, action?, name?, error?}]}`.

### Reading children

`lead_get` / `lead_get_bulk` return the profile children (`custom_plan_profile`, `custom_lab_profile`) inline, filtered to your allowed fields. You can't read the child doctypes directly via `/api/resource/*` — they're child tables with no standalone permission scope; always read them through the parent lead.

tags.name: Files
tags.description:
## Files attached to leads

Files (lab reports PDFs, prescriptions, insurance docs, etc.) attach to leads via Frappe's core `File` doctype. The pattern is:

1. **Upload** the binary using `POST /api/method/upload_file` with multipart/form-data. You tell it `doctype=CRM Lead` + `docname=<lead name>` so it attaches to the right lead. **Always set `is_private=1` for patient documents.**
2. **List** a lead's files by querying `/api/resource/File` filtered by `attached_to_doctype` + `attached_to_name`.
3. **Download** the file by GETting its `file_url`. Public files (`/files/...`) are open; private files (`/private/files/...`) require an authenticated session with read permission on the parent lead.
4. **Delete** with `DELETE /api/resource/File/{name}`.

### Privacy rule — patient docs must be private

Public files at `/files/...` are world-readable to anyone with the URL. Patient lab reports / prescriptions / claim documents must be uploaded with `is_private=1` so they land at `/private/files/...` and require auth to fetch.

A file uploaded as private:
- Returns `file_url: /private/files/<hash>.<ext>` in the response.
- Can only be GETted by a session user who has read permission on the lead it's attached to. Your integration key (with System Manager role) can fetch it. An anonymous tab cannot.

### Download requires the right context

If you fetch a private `file_url` from an integration script, send the same `Authorization: token KEY:SECRET` header. The 403 you'd otherwise get is intentional — the file is gated on lead permission.

tags.name: Reference Data
tags.description:
## Link-master tables — read these to validate your writes

Many lead fields are **Link** fields, meaning the value MUST exist as a row in a lookup master doctype. Sending an unknown value returns HTTP 404 `LinkValidationError`.

Read each master once at startup (or cache) so you know the valid values to send.

| Field on CRM Lead | Lookup master | Example endpoint |
|---|---|---|
| `status` | `CRM Lead Status` | `GET /api/resource/CRM%20Lead%20Status` |
| `source` | `CRM Lead Source` | `GET /api/resource/CRM%20Lead%20Source` |
| `lost_reason` | `CRM Lost Reason` | `GET /api/resource/CRM%20Lost%20Reason` |
| `custom_vertical` | `CRM Vertical` | `GET /api/resource/CRM%20Vertical` |
| `custom_group` | `CRM Group` | `GET /api/resource/CRM%20Group` |
| `custom_current_program` / `custom_previous_program` | `CRM Program` | `GET /api/resource/CRM%20Program` |
| `custom_origin_vertical` | `CRM Vertical` | (same as above) |
| `lead_owner` | `User` (Frappe core) | `GET /api/resource/User?filters=[["enabled","=",1]]&fields=["name","full_name"]` |
| `substage` (on Plan Profile) | `CRM Lead Substage` | `GET /api/resource/CRM%20Lead%20Substage?filters=[["program","=","Niva Bupa"]]` |

Responses are the standard `{"data":[{"name":"..."},...]}` shape — the `name` field of each row is the value you'd send back in your write.

### Substages are program-scoped

Unlike the other lookups, `CRM Lead Substage` rows are scoped by a `program` field. **Always filter by `program=<the lead's current program>`** when enumerating valid substages for a specific lead:

```bash
curl -G -H 'Authorization: token KEY:SECRET' \
     --data-urlencode 'filters=[["program","=","Sigrima"]]' \
     --data-urlencode 'fields=["name","substage_name","main_stage"]' \
     --data-urlencode 'limit_page_length=200' \
     https://one.tatvacare.in/api/resource/CRM%20Lead%20Substage
```

The `name` of each row (format `{program}-{substage_name}`, e.g. `Sigrima-Patient Ready for Chemo`) is what you write back into `custom_plan_profile[].substage`.

tags.name: Schema
tags.description:
## Runtime schema discovery — don't hardcode field lists

The contract for what YOUR key may send/read is **`lead_schema`**, not a hardcoded field list. Call it in your integration code so you adapt automatically and never guess.

### `lead_schema` — your fields, per key (primary)

```bash
curl -H 'Authorization: token <api_key>:<api_secret>' \
     https://one.tatvacare.in/api/method/tatva_connect.api.partner.lead_schema
```

It returns exactly the parent + child fields your key may send/read — each with `fieldname`, `label`, `type`, `required`, and `options` (for Link/Select) — plus your `routing` mode (`forced` for partners, `caller-supplied` for internal) and the bulk/list limits. Two partners get different lists, driven by their Allowed Fields grid. This is your source of truth; the field tables in these docs are illustrative.

### Read a doctype's full definition (internal keys)

> System Manager keys only — partner keys get 403 on `/api/resource/*`.

Every Frappe doctype is itself accessible via REST. Get the full schema (every field, type, options, validations) with:

```bash
curl -H 'Authorization: token KEY:SECRET' \
     https://one.tatvacare.in/api/resource/DocType/CRM%20Lead
```

The response includes a `data.fields[]` array — each entry is one field with attributes `fieldname`, `fieldtype` (`Data`/`Select`/`Link`/`Date`/`Datetime`/`Int`/`Float`/`Check`/`Currency`/`Small Text`/`Long Text`/`Table`/`Section Break`/`Column Break`/`Tab Break`/etc.), `label`, `reqd` (required), `options` (for Link: the target doctype name; for Select: newline-separated valid values), `permlevel` (0=anyone, 1=manager-only), and more.

Same call works for the children — replace the name in the URL (URL-encode spaces):

```
GET /api/resource/DocType/CRM%20Plan%20Profile
GET /api/resource/DocType/CRM%20Lab%20Profile
GET /api/resource/DocType/CRM%20Care%20Providers%20Profile
GET /api/resource/DocType/CRM%20Lead%20Substage
```

### List records of any doctype (internal keys)

For partner keys, list leads via `lead_list` (see [Read](/docs/api/leads)). System Manager keys may also use the raw `/api/resource/<DocType>` pattern:

```bash
curl -H 'Authorization: token <api_key>:<api_secret>' \
     -G --data-urlencode 'filters=[["custom_group","=","Anaya"]]' \
        --data-urlencode 'fields=["name","first_name","mobile_no","status"]' \
        --data-urlencode 'limit_page_length=50' \
     https://one.tatvacare.in/api/resource/CRM%20Lead
```

### When schema changes happen

When we add a field, you'll see it appear in the doctype definition. Your code keeps working — your existing writes succeed (new field is optional and nullable), your reads return the extra field which you can ignore until you're ready to consume it. We notify integrators via email before any **breaking** change (renames, deletes, validation tightening).

### Beyond Lead

The entire Frappe REST pattern works for ANY doctype. If you discover a need (e.g. read `CRM Call Log` records linked to a lead), the same `/api/resource/<DocType>` pattern applies. The doctypes worth knowing about for an integrator:

- `CRM Lead` (this spec's primary target)
- `CRM Plan Profile`, `CRM Lab Profile`, `CRM Care Providers Profile` (children of Lead)
- `CRM Lead Substage` (program-scoped substage master)
- `CRM Vertical`, `CRM Group`, `CRM Program` (routing masters)
- `CRM Lead Status`, `CRM Lead Source`, `CRM Lost Reason` (universal lookup masters)
- `CRM Call Log`, `CRM Task`, `FCRM Note`, `Comment`, `Communication`, `ToDo` (activity-adjacent — read about them in Frappe's standard docs)
- `File` (attachments — see [Attachments](/docs/api/files))

If you find yourself reaching for a non-Lead doctype, email Ops first — we want to know what use case you're hitting so we can document it (or wrap it in a saner endpoint).

tags.name: WhatsApp
tags.description:
## WhatsApp on a lead

> **Rollout note.** Ships with the WATI integration; a `404` means the integration app isn't deployed on that environment yet.

Every WhatsApp message — inbound or outbound — is a row in the **`WhatsApp Message`** doctype, linked to its lead via `reference_doctype` + `reference_name`. So you read a conversation the same way you read anything else: a filtered `GET /api/resource/WhatsApp Message`.

Sending is doctype-driven too — inserting a `WhatsApp Message` row triggers the actual send behind the scenes, routed through **WATI** (our WhatsApp provider) so the team's existing templates and broadcasts keep working. Multi-sender is built in: one **`WhatsApp Account`** row per WhatsApp number, so different product lines can send from different numbers.

See [WhatsApp](/docs/whatsapp) in the Documentation tab for the full picture.

tags.name: Telephony
tags.description:
## Calls on a lead

Calls are rows in **`CRM Call Log`**, linked to a lead via `reference_doctype` + `reference_docname`. **Reading call logs is live today.** A row carries `type` (Incoming/Outgoing), `status`, `from`/`to`, `duration`, and `recording_url` (private — patient PII).

Outbound **click-to-call** and automatic inbound logging are provided by **Acefone**; that half is in build, so the click-to-call endpoint is badged planned and `telephony_medium` has no `Acefone` value until it deploys.

See [Telephony](/docs/telephony) in the Documentation tab.

tags.name: Users
tags.description:
## Users, onboarding & API keys

Every account — an agent or an integration — is a `User`, and its **roles** decide what it can do ([Roles & permissions](/docs/api/roles-and-permissions)). Two admin jobs live here:

- **Onboard** — create a user with the right roles (and, for people, a welcome email to set a password).
- **Mint a key** — generate an `api_key`/`api_secret` for an integration account, exactly the pair [Authentication](/docs/api/authentication) expects.

The end-to-end onboarding of an integration: `POST User` (with its role) → `generate_keys` (capture the secret) → hand the pair to the integration. See [Users & onboarding](/docs/users) in the Documentation tab.

> **⚠ Admin-only.** These need an admin-roled key, and `generate_keys` **rotates** — it invalidates the user's previous secret.

tags.name: Roles and Permissions
tags.description:
## Inspect roles, scoping, and permissions

A key acts **as a user** and runs that user's permission checks — it never bypasses them (see [Permissions in 60 seconds](/docs/frappe-permissions)). What a key can do is decided by three layers:

- **Roles** — what actions (Read / Write / Create / Delete) on what doctypes.
- **User Permissions** — which rows a user may see (row-level scoping, e.g. only `Anaya` leads).
- **Field permlevels** — which fields a user may write (routing fields are level-1, manager-only).

These endpoints let you read all three so you know exactly what a key is allowed to do. See [Roles & permissions](/docs/roles-and-permissions) in the Documentation tab.

tags.name: Tags
tags.description:
## Label leads and find them fast

Tags are free-form labels on a lead (`vip`, `reactivation`, …) — the quickest way to slice leads without adding a field. Frappe keeps them in two synced places: a denormalised `_user_tags` string on the lead (good for a quick `like` filter) and a **`Tag Link`** row per (tag, document) pair (the clean way to ask *which* leads carry a tag). The label vocabulary lives in the **`Tag`** doctype.

Always set/clear tags through the whitelisted `add_tag` / `remove_tag` methods so both stores stay consistent — never write `_user_tags` by hand. See [Tags](/docs/tags) in the Documentation tab.

tags.name: Jobs
tags.description: Scheduled and background jobs. `Scheduled Job Type` holds the definitions (method, cron, stopped, last run); `Scheduled Job Log` holds the execution history. Both are read-only over the API and require System Manager. There is no REST endpoint to trigger a job (Desk / bench only). See the Jobs guide for the inventory.

tags.name: Errors & Logs
tags.description: Operational visibility, read-only. Error Log = server exceptions/tracebacks; Integration Request = outbound provider calls + responses; plus Scheduled Job Log (see Jobs) and WhatsApp Notification Log. Requires System Manager. See the Errors & status guide for a health sweep.

tags.name: Automations
tags.description: Event-driven lead-lifecycle automations: dedup, auto-assignment, tasks, checklists, and the automation registry. See the Internal Automations guide.

apiKeyAuth.name: Authorization
apiKeyAuth.description: Format: `token <api_key>:<api_secret>` — literal lowercase `token`, single space, key:secret joined by `:`. See the [Authentication](/docs/api/authentication) page for how to request a key and a one-liner to verify it works.

CRMLeadUniversal.description: Universal fields on `CRM Lead` itself. 29 custom fields plus standard Frappe fields. Profile children (`custom_plan_profile`, `custom_lab_profile`, `custom_care_providers_profile`) are pointers to embedded child arrays — see [Concepts](/docs/concepts) for the model.

name.description: Frappe primary key, autonamed `CRM-LEAD-YYYY-NNNNN`. **Read-only** — never include in POST bodies (server generates).

first_name.description: Patient first name. Set to `(no name)` if you genuinely don't have it (`lead_create` default-fills this if omitted).

mobile_no.description: **Required** — the dedup anchor (with product line + group). E.164 format: `+<country code><digits>`, no hyphens, no spaces.

phone.description: Secondary phone (optional). E.164 same as mobile_no.

custom_alternate_number.description: Alternate contact phone (optional). E.164 same as mobile_no.

status.description: **Required on create.** Universal lifecycle status. New leads start as `New`. Junk and Unqualified are 'Lost' type and require `lost_reason`. Enumerate live values via [Lookups → Lead Status](/docs/api/reference-data).

source.description: Lead acquisition channel. Link to `CRM Lead Source`. Enumerate via [Lookups → Lead Source](/docs/api/reference-data).

lead_owner.description: Email of the CRM user owning this lead. Link to `User`. **Only set if the email is a real Frappe user** — otherwise leave blank to avoid `LinkValidationError`.

lost_reason.description: **Required when `status` is `Junk` or `Unqualified`.** Link to `CRM Lost Reason`.

lost_notes.description: Free-text note explaining why the lead was lost.

custom_vertical.description: **Permission-locked** (`permlevel=1`). Top-level product line. Link to `CRM Vertical`. See [Concepts › routing chain](/docs/concepts).

custom_group.description: **Permission-locked.** Sub-line under the vertical. Link to `CRM Group`.

custom_current_program.description: The PRIMARY program. Link to `CRM Program`. **`permlevel=0`** — rep-writable, so a rep can self-handoff a program within their line (only `custom_vertical` + `custom_group` are manager-locked at `permlevel=1`). Multi-program patients are supported via profile-row presence (see [Concepts](/docs/concepts)).

custom_previous_program.description: **Permission-locked.** The lead's previous program if they transitioned. Link to `CRM Program`. Captures the LAST switch only — full history is in the (deferred) activity layer.

custom_origin_vertical.description: **Permission-locked. Write-once on capture, never updated.** Records the vertical the patient first entered through, even if their current vertical changes later.

custom_source_origin.description: Where the lead source data originated (deprecated for new writes — use `source` instead).

custom_program_lifecycle_stage.description: **Permission-locked.** Cross-program lifecycle marker (Acquisition / Active / Retention / etc.) — used for cohort analytics.

custom_patient_id.description: External patient identifier from the source system (e.g. MyTatva user ID, partner system ID).

custom_lsq_prospect_id.description: Legacy LeadSquared ProspectId — migration-only dedup key. Not populated for new leads after the LSQ cutover.

custom_lsq_lead_stage_legacy.description: Legacy LeadSquared stage. Read-only for reporting.

custom_lead_gen_id.description: External lead-gen system identifier (campaign or form submission ID).

custom_patient_age.description: Patient age in years. Not auto-calculated from DOB today — caller-provided.

custom_caregiver_relation.description: Caregiver's relationship to patient (e.g. Spouse, Daughter, Son).

custom_plan_profile.description: **Embedded child rows** (max 1 today). Plan / PSP / subscription / drug-program / payment / acquisition / dx-funnel data. On PUT, the array REPLACES the existing rows — see [Write › profile semantics](/docs/api/leads).

custom_lab_profile.description: Embedded child rows. Lab values, vitals, conditions, scores.

custom_care_providers_profile.description: Embedded child rows. Care team — coaches, RM, therapist, doctor, hospital.

modified.description: **Read-only.** Last-modified timestamp. Useful for change-tracking / delta sync.

PlanProfile.description: Child of `CRM Lead`. 79 fields. Holds everything plan-shaped: subscription / PSP enrollment, drug-program clinical (oncology), Rx workflow, payment + insurer fields, acquisition (UTM + signup), diagnostic-funnel timestamps, and the program-scoped substage Link. See [Concepts](/docs/concepts) for why this is sliced by concern, not by program.

signup_date.description: Date the patient signed up (any platform).

mobile_app_signup_date.description: Date specifically of mobile app sign-up.

signed_up_on_app.description: Boolean flag (0/1). Whether the patient signed up via mobile app.

platform.description: Mobile app platform — Android / iOS / Web.

user_source.description: Free-text source identifier (granular than `CRM Lead Source`).

utm_disease.description: UTM disease tag — used in disease-targeted campaigns.

source_campaign.description: Source campaign (alternate naming used by some campaigns).

plan_name.description: Name of the plan / PSP / subscription the patient is enrolled in.

plan_includes_cgm.description: Boolean. Whether the plan includes a Continuous Glucose Monitor.

bca_device.description: Body Composition Analyzer device, if part of the plan.

dx_landing_at.description: Diagnostic funnel step: patient landed on the lab-test booking page. Stamp current time when the LSQ flag fires.

dx_intent_at.description: Diagnostic funnel step: patient indicated intent to book.

dx_details_at.description: Diagnostic funnel step: patient submitted personal details.

dx_address_at.description: Diagnostic funnel step: patient submitted address.

dx_slot_at.description: Diagnostic funnel step: patient selected a slot.

labtest_booked_at.description: Lab test booking completed (terminal funnel step).

invoice_uploaded.description: Boolean. Whether the patient has uploaded an invoice.

payment_link.description: URL the patient can use to complete payment.

paying_status.description: Free-text payment status (e.g. Paid, Pending, Defaulted).

policy_number.description: Insurer policy number (e.g. Niva Bupa policy).

member_id.description: Insurer member identifier within the policy.

physio_or_dietician.description: Boolean. Whether the plan includes physio / dietician.

transition_from_sigrima.description: Transition marker if patient came from Sigrima.

transition_from_ujvira.description: Transition marker if patient came from Ujvira.

transition_old_to_new_pap.description: Transition from old PAP to new PAP.

rx_status.description: Prescription review status (e.g. Pending / Approved / Rejected).

rx_reviewed_by.description: Email of the user who reviewed the prescription. Link to `User`.

rx_rejection_reason.description: Reason the prescription was rejected (if applicable).

substage.description: **Program-scoped operational stage.** Link to `CRM Lead Substage`. Value format is `{program}-{substage_name}` (e.g. `Niva Bupa-Payment Link Sent`, `Sigrima-Patient Ready for Chemo`). Enumerate valid values for a program via [Lookups › Lead Substage](/docs/api/reference-data). The universal `status` field on `CRM Lead` is independent of substage — see [Concepts › substages](/docs/concepts).

LabProfile.description: Child of `CRM Lead`. 22 fields. Lab values, vitals (height/weight/BMI), conditions (primary + co-morbid), and metabolic score / assessment data.

bmi.description: Body Mass Index. Caller-computed today (no auto-calc hook yet). Formula: `weight_kg / (height_meters^2)`.

primary_condition.description: Patient's primary medical condition. Free text today; will become a Link master when the option list is locked.

comorbid_conditions.description: Comma-separated co-morbid conditions. Will move to a child Table doctype when multi-select pattern is needed.

metabolic_questionnaire_link.description: URL to the metabolic questionnaire PDF / form.

assessment_responses.description: Raw assessment payload — typically a JSON-as-string blob with all questionnaire responses.

report_date.description: Date the lab report was generated (`YYYY-MM-DD`).

CareProvidersProfile.description: Child of `CRM Lead`. 15 fields. The patient's care team (internal coaches + RM + therapist) and medical providers (treating doctor + hospital). UI label: 'Care & Providers'. The internal doctype name has no `&` (Frappe restriction).

success_coach_name.description: Success Coach name — primary internal contact for the patient.

rm_name.description: Relationship Manager name — handles calls and follow-ups.

abm_name.description: Area Business Manager — operational lead handler for PSP-style engagements.

coach_appointment_booked_at.description: Timestamp when the patient's first coach appointment was booked.

LookupItem.description: Generic single-column lookup-master row. The `name` field holds the value you'd send back into a Link field on `CRM Lead`.

name.description: Primary key — also the human-readable value (e.g. `Sigrima`, `Anaya`, `Mobile App`).

LeadSubstage.description: Row in `CRM Lead Substage` master. One row per (program, substage_name) pair. New programs add rows here — never new columns on Plan Profile.

name.description: Auto-formatted `{program}-{substage_name}`. This is the value you send back into `custom_plan_profile[].substage`.

substage_name.description: Human-readable substage name within a program.

program.description: Link to `CRM Program`. Which program this substage belongs to.

UpsertLeadRequest.description: Request body for `POST /api/method/tatva_connect.api.partner.lead_create`. Only `mobile_no` is strictly required (the dedup anchor); `first_name` and `status` are default-filled if missing (`(no name)` and `New`). All other fields are optional and must be within the fields your key is allowed to send (call `lead_schema` to see them). Partners never send routing — it's forced server-side. Profile children embed as arrays and merge field-by-field.

UpsertLeadResponse.description: Success response from `lead_create` / `lead_update`. The payload is wrapped in `message` (Frappe method convention). A validation/permission failure raises a normal Frappe error (non-2xx) with the usual error shape — see [Errors](/docs/errors).

action.description: Whether the lead was created or upserted onto an existing one.

name.description: CRM Lead primary key (e.g. `CRM-LEAD-2026-00123`). PUT this back to `lead_update`.

source.description: The lead's source (forced from your mapping for partners).

FrappeListResponse.description: Standard Frappe list response shape. `data` is an array of objects with the fields requested via the `fields` query parameter.

FrappeDocResponse.description: Standard Frappe single-document response. `data` is the full doc with all fields (including embedded child arrays for Tables).

FileDoc.description: Row in Frappe's core `File` doctype. Returned by upload, list, and get operations.

name.description: Frappe primary key for the file row (hash-based, e.g. `c14b27639e`).

file_url.description: Path to download. `/files/<x>` for public, `/private/files/<x>` for private.

is_private.description: 0 = public (open to anyone with URL), 1 = private (auth required).

attached_to_doctype.description: Parent doctype this file is attached to.

content_hash.description: MD5 of the content — use to dedup re-uploads.

Error.description: Frappe error envelope. Returned for HTTP 4xx/5xx — including a `lead_create` / `lead_update` failure (validation, permission, unknown Link value), which raises a normal Frappe error rather than a 200.

exc_type.description: Exception class name. Use to programmatically discriminate error types.

exception.example: Could not find Custom Field: CRM Lead-foo

_server_messages.description: JSON-encoded array of additional human-readable messages.

CallLog.description: One row in `CRM Call Log` — a single inbound or outbound call.

telephony_medium.description: Provider that placed the call (`Manual` / `Twilio` / `Exotel`; `Acefone` once deployed).

WhatsAppMessage.description: One row in `WhatsApp Message` — a single inbound or outbound WhatsApp message (planned surface).

content_type.description: text / image / document / video / audio / interactive / template.

message_id.description: Threading id used to match status updates.

whatsapp_account.description: Sending account / WhatsApp number (Link to WhatsApp Account).

UserPermission.description: One row-level restriction — limits which records a user may see.

allow.description: The doctype the restriction is on.

for_value.description: The single value the user is scoped to.

applicable_for.description: Restrict only when accessing this doctype (blank = everywhere).

UserAccount.description: A Frappe `User` — an agent or an integration account. Roles decide capability.

api_key.description: Set once keys are generated; the secret is never returned on read.

BulkResultEntry.description: One record's outcome in a bulk response. `ok` tells you whether that single record succeeded; `action`/`name` are present on success, `error` on failure.

index.description: 0-based position of this record in the request array.

ok.description: True if this record succeeded (its own savepoint committed).

action.description: What happened to this record (present only on success).

name.description: CRM Lead id (present only on success).

error.description: Failure message for this record (present only on failure).

BulkResponse.description: Partial-success envelope shared by all bulk write methods. Each record runs in its own savepoint — one bad row reports its error, the rest still commit. Payload wrapped in `message` (Frappe method convention).

ok.description: Always true — the batch ran (individual rows may have failed).

failed.description: Records that were rolled back and reported.

CuratedLead.description: A lead as the caller is allowed to see it: their allowed parent fields plus the read-only identity/routing block (`name`, `source`, `custom_vertical`, `custom_group`, `custom_current_program`). `lead_get` / `lead_get_bulk` additionally inline the profile children (filtered to allowed fields); `lead_list` returns no children.

LeadUpdateRequest.description: Request body for `lead_update`. Carries the CRM `name` (the id `lead_create` returned) plus any allowed fields to change. Parent fields you SEND are updated, fields you OMIT are left alone; profile children merge field-by-field. Partners may not change routing (it's forced).

MinimalUpsert.summary: Minimum viable — just enough to create a lead
MinimalUpsert.description: Only the dedup key (`mobile_no`) plus mandatory `first_name`/`status` (or let them default-fill). No routing, no profiles. Useful for an absolute-minimum smoke test.

UpsertWithRouting.summary: Minimum + routing — locked to a program
UpsertWithRouting.description: Adds the 4 routing fields. Lead is now tagged to a specific vertical / group / program, which drives sales-rep assignment and reporting.

UpsertWithOneProfile.summary: Add one profile child — lab values for an insurer lead
UpsertWithOneProfile.description: Now we attach a Lab Profile row. Typical of an insurer pushing a lab-screened lead.

InsurerLabIntakeLead.summary: Full — insurer-screening lead (labs + policy + alt phone)
InsurerLabIntakeLead.description: Realistic shape for an inbound insurer feed. Plan Profile for policy/payment, Lab Profile for the full panel.

OncologyPSPLead.summary: Full — oncology PSP lead (drug program + care team)
OncologyPSPLead.description: Realistic shape for an Anaya Sigrima clinical lead. Plan Profile has drug-specific fields + the program substage; Care & Providers has the assigned coach + doctor + hospital.

WellnessSubscriptionLead.summary: Full — wellness subscription lead (plan + score + care team)
WellnessSubscriptionLead.description: Realistic shape for a MyTatva-style subscription sign-up. Plan Profile carries subscription + UTM; Lab Profile carries metabolic score; Care & Providers carries the assigned coach stack.

UpsertSuccessUpdated.summary: lead_create response — updated (existing lead found on the same line)

value.exception: Please specify a reason for losing the lead.
Could not find CRM Program: SigrimaXXX

LookupSubstageResponse.summary: Lead Substage lookup — filtered by program

LeadSchemaResponse.summary: lead_schema — a partner with a labs+plan grid

message.child_write: Send each child as a JSON array under its key, e.g. custom_lab_profile=[{...}]. The existing row is merged field-by-field.
message.dedup: A lead is unique per (mobile_no, product line, group). Re-sending the same trio updates that lead; a different line creates a new one.

routing.note: Your routing is fixed. Any routing fields you send are ignored.

LeadSchemaResponseInternal.summary: lead_schema — a trusted internal (System Manager) key

message.child_write: Send each child as a JSON array under its key, e.g. custom_lab_profile=[{...}]. The existing row is merged field-by-field.
message.dedup: A lead is unique per (mobile_no, product line, group). Re-sending the same trio updates that lead; a different line creates a new one.

routing.note: Trusted caller: send these routing fields in the body.

CuratedLeadGet.summary: lead_get — one curated lead with children

LeadUpdateRequest.summary: lead_update — flip status on a known lead

LeadListResponse.summary: lead_list — page of curated leads (no children)

get.description:
Returns the email of the user the API key belongs to. The single fastest way to verify your `Authorization` header is correct.

**Use this** as your first call when integrating, and as a health-check in your CI/CD pipeline. Anything other than HTTP 200 with the user's email means your auth is broken before any business call.

401.description: Bad/missing API key. Body returns HTML (Frappe website login redirect) — that's the signal.

get.description:
Standard Frappe list endpoint. Returns the requested `fields` for leads matching `filters`, paginated.

Filter operator vocabulary, pagination, and `--data-urlencode` curl pattern are explained in [Read](/docs/api/leads).

parameters.name: filters
parameters.description: JSON-encoded array of `[field, operator, value]` filters. AND'd together.

parameters.name: fields
parameters.description: JSON-encoded array of fieldnames to return. Defaults to `name` only — usually you want more.

parameters.name: order_by
parameters.description: SQL-style ORDER BY. Useful: `modified desc` for delta-sync.

post.description:
Standard Frappe create. **Internal System Manager keys only** — partner keys get 403 here and must use `lead_create`. **Prefer `lead_create`** anyway unless you specifically need create-only semantics (e.g. you've verified the lead doesn't exist and want a hard error if it does).

Unlike `lead_create`: not idempotent on the lead grain, will not merge with an existing lead, and does not enforce the partner allowed-fields/routing rules.

404.description: LinkValidationError — a Link value isn't in its master.

get.description: Returns the full lead doc, INCLUDING all child Table fields (`custom_plan_profile`, `custom_lab_profile`, `custom_care_providers_profile`) inline as arrays. Each array is `[]` if no row exists, otherwise `[{...}]` with all fields.

put.description:
Send only changed fields. **Internal System Manager keys only** — partner keys get 403 here and must use `lead_update`.

### Profile child semantics

A raw PUT with `custom_plan_profile: [{...}]` **REPLACES** the entire array. To update a single field on the existing row without losing the rest, use **read-modify-write**:

```python
lead = GET /api/resource/CRM Lead/{name}
row  = (lead.custom_plan_profile or [{}])[0]
row['dx_landing_at'] = '2026-06-02 10:15:00'
PUT /api/resource/CRM Lead/{name}  body={'custom_plan_profile': [row]}
```

**Or just use [`lead_update`](/docs/api/leads)** — it merges your fields into the existing child row automatically (no read-modify-write needed).

delete.description: Hard delete. Use sparingly. **For lifecycle-end leads, prefer setting `status` to `Junk` or `Unqualified` instead** — preserves history for reporting.

parameters.name: name
parameters.description: CRM Lead primary key. Obtained from a prior write response (`message.name` from `lead_create`) or a list query.

get.description: Returns the number of records matching the filters. Useful for pre-write checks (e.g. duplicate phone count before insert) and dashboard counts.

get.summary: Discover the fields your key may use
get.description:
**Discovery — call this in your integration code instead of hardcoding a field list.** Returns exactly the parent + child fields THIS key may send/read (each with `fieldname`, `label`, `type`, `required`, `options`), the child-write/dedup notes, the bulk/list limits, and your `routing` mode. Two partners get **different** lists, driven by their Allowed Fields grid. `routing.mode` is `forced` for partners (with the fixed line) and `caller-supplied` for trusted internal keys.

> **Partner vs internal.** A **partner** key has its routing **forced** from its `Lead API Mapping` row, is **scoped** to its own line (vertical + group), and may only send/read the fields ticked in its **Allowed Fields** grid. A **trusted internal** key (System Manager, no mapping) sends routing in the body, sees all lines, and uses the full catalog. **Partner keys get 403 on raw `/api/resource/*`** — these `partner.*` methods are their entire surface.

200.description: The caller's allowed fields + routing mode (wrapped in `message`).

403.description: Caller is neither a partner with a Lead API Mapping nor a System Manager.

get.summary: Read one lead by name or mobile_no
get.description:
Fetch ONE lead by `name` **or** `mobile_no`. Returns only the caller's allowed fields plus the read-only routing block, with profile children (`custom_lab_profile`, `custom_plan_profile`) inlined and filtered to allowed fields. A partner only sees leads on their own line; a lookup outside scope returns 404 `DoesNotExistError`. Pass exactly one of `name` / `mobile_no`.

> **Partner vs internal.** A **partner** key has its routing **forced** from its `Lead API Mapping` row, is **scoped** to its own line (vertical + group), and may only send/read the fields ticked in its **Allowed Fields** grid. A **trusted internal** key (System Manager, no mapping) sends routing in the body, sees all lines, and uses the full catalog. **Partner keys get 403 on raw `/api/resource/*`** — these `partner.*` methods are their entire surface.

parameters.name: name
parameters.description: CRM Lead id (the value `lead_create` returned). Use this OR `mobile_no`.

parameters.name: mobile_no
parameters.description: 10-digit Indian number (auto-normalised to `+91…`) or an already-`+`-prefixed number. Use this OR `name`.

403.description: Not authorised (no Lead API Mapping and not a System Manager), or the lead is outside your line.

get.description:
List leads on the caller's line, filtered + paginated. Honours a **curated, safe set of filters only** (never arbitrary fields) and always auto-scopes to your line. Returns curated parent fields, **no children** — use `lead_get` for the full record. Page size defaults to 20, max 200; page with `offset`.

> **Partner vs internal.** A **partner** key has its routing **forced** from its `Lead API Mapping` row, is **scoped** to its own line (vertical + group), and may only send/read the fields ticked in its **Allowed Fields** grid. A **trusted internal** key (System Manager, no mapping) sends routing in the body, sees all lines, and uses the full catalog. **Partner keys get 403 on raw `/api/resource/*`** — these `partner.*` methods are their entire surface.

parameters.name: offset
parameters.description: Rows to skip (paging). Alias `limit_start` is also accepted.

200.description: A page of curated leads (no children), wrapped in `message`.

403.description: Not authorised (no Lead API Mapping and not a System Manager).

post.description:
**The default write endpoint for ~99% of integrations.** Idempotent on the lead grain (`mobile_no` + product line + group). Safe to retry. Finds the existing lead matching that trio and updates it, or creates a new one. Returns the CRM `name` — PUT it back to `lead_update`. Profile children are passed as embedded arrays.

### What you may send

Only the fields YOUR key is allowed to send — call `lead_schema` to see them. A partner's routing (`source` / `vertical` / `group` / `program`) is **forced** from their `Lead API Mapping` row; any routing fields a partner sends are silently ignored. A trusted System Manager key sends routing in the body. Fields outside your allowed set are dropped.

### How it merges

For an existing lead, parent fields you SEND are updated; fields you OMIT are LEFT ALONE. Profile children **merge field-by-field** into the existing row (a partial write never wipes the row).

### Default-fill behaviour

- Missing `first_name` → set to `(no name)`
- Missing `status` → set to `New`
- Profile arrays accept either actual JSON arrays or stringified JSON.

### Common pitfalls

- Sending an unknown Link value (e.g. mistyped program name) → `LinkValidationError`. Fix: read [Lookups](/docs/api/reference-data) first.
- Setting `status` to `Junk` or `Unqualified` without `lost_reason` → `ValidationError: Please specify a reason for losing the lead.`
- Sending fields outside your allowed set → silently dropped (not an error). Confirm with `lead_schema`.

### Related

[Read › Get one lead](/docs/api/leads) to fetch the lead back after writing. [Lookups](/docs/api/reference-data) to enumerate valid Link values before writing.

200.description: Lead created or upserted. Payload under `message`.

403.description: Caller is neither a partner with a Lead API Mapping nor a System Manager (or a partner reached outside their line).

post.description:
Create-or-update up to **100** leads in one call. Body: `{"leads":[{...}, …]}`. Each record uses the same idempotent phone+line dedup and field rules as `lead_create`, and runs in its own savepoint — **partial success**: one bad row reports its error, the rest still commit. Over 100 records returns a clean error (page the rest).

> **Partner vs internal.** A **partner** key has its routing **forced** from its `Lead API Mapping` row, is **scoped** to its own line (vertical + group), and may only send/read the fields ticked in its **Allowed Fields** grid. A **trusted internal** key (System Manager, no mapping) sends routing in the body, sees all lines, and uses the full catalog. **Partner keys get 403 on raw `/api/resource/*`** — these `partner.*` methods are their entire surface.

leads.description: Up to 100 lead payloads (same shape as `lead_create`).

403.description: Not authorised (no Lead API Mapping and not a System Manager).

post.summary: Read many leads by names or mobile_nos
post.description:
Read up to **100** leads in one call by `names` **or** `mobile_nos` (send one). Returns the curated leads found (allowed fields + read-only routing + inlined children). Out-of-scope or missing ids are simply omitted — `found` may be less than `requested`. Uses POST because the id list travels in the body.

> **Partner vs internal.** A **partner** key has its routing **forced** from its `Lead API Mapping` row, is **scoped** to its own line (vertical + group), and may only send/read the fields ticked in its **Allowed Fields** grid. A **trusted internal** key (System Manager, no mapping) sends routing in the body, sees all lines, and uses the full catalog. **Partner keys get 403 on raw `/api/resource/*`** — these `partner.*` methods are their entire surface.

names.description: Up to 100 CRM Lead ids. Use this OR `mobile_nos`.

mobile_nos.description: Up to 100 phone numbers (auto-normalised). Use this OR `names`.

403.description: Not authorised (no Lead API Mapping and not a System Manager).

put.summary: Update a lead by CRM name
put.description:
Update an existing lead identified by its CRM `name` (the id `lead_create` returned). Send `name` plus any allowed fields; parent fields you SEND are updated, fields you OMIT are LEFT ALONE, and profile children **merge field-by-field** (a partial write never wipes the row). A partner is scope-checked — updating a lead on another line returns 403, and a partner can never move a lead to a different line (routing is forced).

> **Partner vs internal.** A **partner** key has its routing **forced** from its `Lead API Mapping` row, is **scoped** to its own line (vertical + group), and may only send/read the fields ticked in its **Allowed Fields** grid. A **trusted internal** key (System Manager, no mapping) sends routing in the body, sees all lines, and uses the full catalog. **Partner keys get 403 on raw `/api/resource/*`** — these `partner.*` methods are their entire surface.

403.description: Not authorised, or the lead is outside your line (partner scope check).

put.description:
Update up to **100** leads in one call. Body: `{"updates":[{"name":…, …fields}, …]}` — each entry needs its CRM `name` plus the fields to change. Same merge semantics and partner scope-check as `lead_update`, applied per record in its own savepoint (**partial success**).

> **Partner vs internal.** A **partner** key has its routing **forced** from its `Lead API Mapping` row, is **scoped** to its own line (vertical + group), and may only send/read the fields ticked in its **Allowed Fields** grid. A **trusted internal** key (System Manager, no mapping) sends routing in the body, sees all lines, and uses the full catalog. **Partner keys get 403 on raw `/api/resource/*`** — these `partner.*` methods are their entire surface.

updates.description: Up to 100 update payloads; each carries `name` + allowed fields.

403.description: Not authorised, or a record is outside your line (per-record partner scope check).

delete.summary: Delete a lead by CRM name
delete.description:
Delete a lead by its CRM `name` (query param or body). A partner is scope-checked — deleting a lead on another line returns 403. A lead with linked activity (calls, tasks, notes) raises `LinkExistsError`, so a worked lead can't be silently nuked.

> **Partner vs internal.** A **partner** key has its routing **forced** from its `Lead API Mapping` row, is **scoped** to its own line (vertical + group), and may only send/read the fields ticked in its **Allowed Fields** grid. A **trusted internal** key (System Manager, no mapping) sends routing in the body, sees all lines, and uses the full catalog. **Partner keys get 403 on raw `/api/resource/*`** — these `partner.*` methods are their entire surface.

parameters.name: name
parameters.description: CRM Lead id to delete (may also be sent in the JSON body).

403.description: Not authorised, or the lead is outside your line (partner scope check).

delete.description:
Delete up to **100** leads by CRM `name`. Body: `{"names":[…]}`. Each delete runs in its own savepoint (**partial success**) and is partner scope-checked; a lead with linked activity fails with `LinkExistsError` for that row while the rest proceed.

> **Partner vs internal.** A **partner** key has its routing **forced** from its `Lead API Mapping` row, is **scoped** to its own line (vertical + group), and may only send/read the fields ticked in its **Allowed Fields** grid. A **trusted internal** key (System Manager, no mapping) sends routing in the body, sees all lines, and uses the full catalog. **Partner keys get 403 on raw `/api/resource/*`** — these `partner.*` methods are their entire surface.

names.description: Up to 100 CRM Lead ids to delete.

403.description: Not authorised, or a record is outside your line (per-record partner scope check).

get.summary: List files attached to a lead
get.description:
Standard Frappe list endpoint for the `File` doctype. To get files for a specific lead, filter on `attached_to_doctype` + `attached_to_name`.

```bash
curl -G -H 'Authorization: token KEY:SECRET' \
     --data-urlencode 'filters=[["attached_to_doctype","=","CRM Lead"],["attached_to_name","=","CRM-LEAD-2026-00123"]]' \
     --data-urlencode 'fields=["name","file_name","file_url","file_size","is_private","creation"]' \
     https://one.tatvacare.in/api/resource/File
```

post.summary: Upload a file and attach to a lead
post.description:
Frappe core file-upload endpoint. Multipart/form-data. **Always set `is_private=1` for patient documents** — the file URL will be `/private/files/...` requiring auth to fetch.

### Required form parts

- `file` — the binary file content
- `doctype` — `CRM Lead`
- `docname` — the lead's primary key (e.g. `CRM-LEAD-2026-00123`)
- `is_private` — `1` (use `1` for patient docs, `0` only for non-sensitive content)

### Optional form parts

- `fieldname` — if you want the file to populate a specific Attach field on the lead
- `folder` — defaults to `Home`
- `file_name` — override the display name
- `optimize` — optimize images on upload (jpg/png/gif)

### Curl example

```bash
curl -X POST -H 'Authorization: token KEY:SECRET' \
     -F 'file=@./lab-report-may.pdf' \
     -F 'doctype=CRM Lead' \
     -F 'docname=CRM-LEAD-2026-00123' \
     -F 'is_private=1' \
     https://one.tatvacare.in/api/method/upload_file
```

### Download the file later

The response includes `file_url`. To download:
- Public files (`/files/...`) — open `GET <base_url><file_url>` with no auth.
- Private files (`/private/files/...`) — `GET <base_url><file_url>` with the same `Authorization: token KEY:SECRET` header. A 403 from a private URL means your auth is missing or the lead permission check failed.

### Dedup re-uploads

Each upload creates a new `File` row, even if you upload the same bytes twice. To dedup client-side, hash the file and check existing files via `GET /api/resource/File?filters=[["content_hash","=","<md5>"],["attached_to_name","=","<lead>"]]`.

delete.description: Removes the File row AND the physical file from disk. Idempotent — a second DELETE returns 404, which is fine.

get.description:
Read a lead's WhatsApp thread. Filter by `reference_doctype` = `CRM Lead` and `reference_name` = the lead's name (note: WhatsApp uses `reference_name`, calls use `reference_docname`).

```bash
curl -G -H 'Authorization: token KEY:SECRET' \
  --data-urlencode 'filters=[["reference_doctype","=","CRM Lead"],["reference_name","=","CRM-LEAD-2026-00123"]]' \
  --data-urlencode 'order_by=creation asc' \
  https://one.tatvacare.in/api/resource/WhatsApp%20Message
```

data.name: a1b2c3d4e5
data.message: Hi, I'd like to know about the Anaya program.

get.description:
> **Status — planned.** This surface ships with the integration rollout; it is **not deployed yet**, so the live server returns `404`. The shape below is the contract you'll integrate against.

Fetch a single message by its Frappe `name`.

data.name: a1b2c3d4e5
data.message: Hi, I'd like to know about the Anaya program.

get.description:
Read the calls linked to a lead. Filter by `reference_doctype` = `CRM Lead` and `reference_docname` = the lead's name.

```bash
curl -G -H 'Authorization: token KEY:SECRET' \
  --data-urlencode 'filters=[["reference_doctype","=","CRM Lead"],["reference_docname","=","CRM-LEAD-2026-00123"]]' \
  --data-urlencode 'fields=["name","type","status","from","to","duration","start_time"]' \
  https://one.tatvacare.in/api/resource/CRM%20Call%20Log
```
Fetch a single call by its Frappe `name`, including `recording_url` and timestamps.

post.description: Ring the agent and bridge to the lead/deal's number via Acefone. Creates an Initiated CRM Call Log; the account is chosen by the lead's Product Line / Group / Program routing (no route -> blocked). The async webhook reconciles the row to a terminal status. Requires System Manager.

get.description:
List agents and integration accounts. Filter to active users; this is also how you resolve a valid `lead_owner` before assigning a lead.

```bash
curl -G -H 'Authorization: token KEY:SECRET' \
  --data-urlencode 'filters=[["enabled","=",1]]' \
  --data-urlencode 'fields=["name","full_name","enabled"]' \
  https://one.tatvacare.in/api/resource/User
```

post.description:
Create a `User`, assigning roles inline (roles are a child table, so set them here rather than via a separate call). Set `send_welcome_email: 1` to trigger Frappe's password-set email.

For an **integration** account, create it with the role(s) it needs, then mint its keys with `generate_keys` below.

get.description:
Fetch a user by email. The returned doc embeds the `roles` child table — the list of roles that decides the account's capabilities (see [Roles & permissions](/docs/api/roles-and-permissions)).

Note: the built-in `Administrator` account is specially protected and returns `403`; ordinary users read fine.

put.description: Partial update. Common admin uses: disable an account (`{"enabled": 0}`) or change its roles. Roles are a child table — send the **full** desired `roles` array (it replaces the existing set).

post.summary: Generate API keys for a user
post.description:
Mint an `api_key` + `api_secret` for a user — this is how an integration account gets the credentials described in [Authentication](/docs/api/authentication).

> **⚠ This rotates the secret.** Calling it **invalidates the user's previous `api_secret`** immediately. The new secret is returned **once, in this response, and never again** — capture it now or you'll have to regenerate (breaking the old one). The `api_key` is set on the user doc; the secret is only ever shown here.

get.description: Every role defined on the site. The `name` of a role is what you'd assign to a user.
Role Profiles bundle several roles under one name, so you can grant a consistent set in one assignment instead of listing roles individually.

Ask whether the **current key's user** has a given permission on a doctype (optionally a specific record). Returns `{"message": {"has_permission": 1}}` or `0`. Use it to fail fast in your integration before attempting a write.

parameters.name: docname
parameters.description: Optional — check on a specific record.

get.description:
The row-level restrictions in force. Filter by `user` to see exactly which records a key's user is scoped to. No rows for a user = unrestricted (a manager who sees everything).

```bash
curl -G -H 'Authorization: token KEY:SECRET' \
  --data-urlencode 'filters=[["user","=","agent-anaya@tatvacare.in"]]' \
  https://one.tatvacare.in/api/resource/User%20Permission
```

post.description: Restrict a user to specific records. Example: scope an agent to only the `Anaya` group — `{"user":"agent@…","allow":"CRM Group","for_value":"Anaya"}`. After this, that user's lead lists silently exclude everything else. Managers (no User Permission rows) keep seeing all.

for_value.description: The single value the user is scoped to.

applicable_for.description: Optional — restrict only when accessing this doctype.

get.description: Read the valid values for `custom_vertical` on `CRM Lead`. Top-level product line.
Read the valid values for `custom_group` on `CRM Lead`. Sub-line under a Vertical (e.g. Anaya, Anyra, Eisai, GoodFlip, Insurers).
Read the valid values for `custom_current_program` / `custom_previous_program` on `CRM Lead`. Named PSP / subscription / SKU.

Read the valid values for `status` on `CRM Lead`. 7 universal statuses: `New` (Open) / `Contacted`, `Nurture` (Ongoing) / `Qualified`, `Converted` (Won) / `Junk`, `Unqualified` (Lost — both require `lost_reason`).
Read the valid values for `source` on `CRM Lead`. Lead acquisition channels.
Read the valid values for `lost_reason` on `CRM Lead`. Required when status is `Junk` or `Unqualified`.

Read the valid values for `substage` on `CRM Plan Profile`. **Always filter by `program`** — substages are scoped per program, and the unfiltered list is the union across all programs (confusing).

The `name` field in each row is the value you send back as `substage` (format `{program}-{substage_name}`).

Returns the live doctype definition: every field with `fieldname`, `fieldtype`, `label`, `reqd`, `options` (Link target or Select options), `permlevel`, etc. See [Discovery](/docs/api/schema) for how to use this in your integration code.

Works for ANY doctype — Lead, profiles, lookups, even Frappe core doctypes like `User` or `File`.
The tag vocabulary — one row per distinct label across the site.

get.summary: Find which leads carry a tag
get.description:
One row per (tag, document) pair. Filter by `tag` to get every lead that carries it — the clean way to pull a tagged cohort.

```bash
curl -G -H 'Authorization: token KEY:SECRET' \
  --data-urlencode 'filters=[["tag","=","vip"],["document_type","=","CRM Lead"]]' \
  --data-urlencode 'fields=["document_name","tag"]' \
  https://one.tatvacare.in/api/resource/Tag%20Link
```

post.description:
Add a tag to a document. Keeps `_user_tags` and `Tag Link` in sync — the only correct way to set a tag.

```bash
curl -X POST -H 'Authorization: token KEY:SECRET' \
  -d 'tag=vip' -d 'dt=CRM Lead' -d 'dn=CRM-LEAD-2026-00123' \
  https://one.tatvacare.in/api/method/frappe.desk.doctype.tag.tag.add_tag
```
Remove a tag from a document. Same arguments as `add_tag`.

post.summary: Send an approved template to a lead
post.description: Send a WATI-approved template to a lead, filling its {{N}} variables. Inserts a `WhatsApp Message` row; the WATI send + status threading run behind it. The account is resolved from the lead's routing (a lead with no route is rejected — never sent through the wrong tenant). Requires System Manager.

body_param.description: JSON object keyed by the real {{N}} index. Omit for a static template.

200.description: Created — returns the new WhatsApp Message name.

post.summary: Refresh a lead's thread from WATI
post.description: Rebuild ONE lead's WhatsApp thread from WATI's authoritative history (getMessages, all pages). Scoped to that lead, idempotent (keyed on WATI's message id), and never re-sends (rows are written directly). Backs the 'Refresh WhatsApp' button on the lead header. Requires System Manager.

Mirror approved WATI templates into the read-only `WhatsApp Templates` catalog. No body syncs EVERY WATI account; pass account_name to sync one. Records are keyed per account (name::Account). Also runs automatically every 6 hours. Requires System Manager.

account_name.description: Optional — omit to sync all WATI accounts.

200.description: OK — returns per-account {created, updated, skipped} counts.

get.description:
Every scheduled job and its schedule. `stopped=1` is disabled; `last_execution` is null if it has never run (e.g. scheduler paused). Filter `method like "%tatva_connect%"` for just the custom jobs.

```bash
curl -G -H 'Authorization: token KEY:SECRET' \
  --data-urlencode 'fields=["name","method","frequency","cron_format","stopped","last_execution"]' \
  --data-urlencode 'limit_page_length=0' \
  'https://one.tatvacare.in/api/resource/Scheduled Job Type'
```
Full definition of a single job, e.g. `templates_sync.scheduled_sync_all`.

Did a job run, and did it fail? `status` is `Complete` / `Failed` / `Scheduled`; a failed row's `details` holds the traceback.

```bash
curl -G -H 'Authorization: token KEY:SECRET' \
  --data-urlencode 'filters=[["scheduled_job_type","=","templates_sync.scheduled_sync_all"]]' \
  --data-urlencode 'order_by=creation desc' \
  --data-urlencode 'limit_page_length=20' \
  'https://one.tatvacare.in/api/resource/Scheduled Job Log'
```

post.description: Create a WhatsApp/WATI account (one per WATI tenant). `token` is a Password field (stored encrypted, never read back). System Manager only.

Map a lead's Product Line / Group / Program to a WhatsApp account. Fill only the axes you want (blank = any); most-specific wins; duplicate triples are rejected. Axis values are names of existing CRM Vertical / CRM Group / CRM Program records. System Manager only.

Create an Acefone account (one per account; carries the agent line, DID, token). `api_token`/`click_to_call_api_key` are Password fields (write-only). `caller_id` is the DID — outbound caller-id and inbound attribution key. System Manager or Sales Manager.
Map a lead's Product Line / Group / Program to an Acefone account. Fill only the axes you want; most-specific wins; duplicate triples are rejected. System Manager only.

get.description:
Server-side exceptions/tracebacks (everything `frappe.log_error` writes). `method` is the error title — filter `method like "%WATI%"` or `%Acefone%` for an integration. Read one by name to get the full traceback in `error`.

```bash
curl -G -H 'Authorization: token KEY:SECRET' \
  --data-urlencode 'fields=["name","method","creation"]' \
  --data-urlencode 'order_by=creation desc' --data-urlencode 'limit_page_length=30' \
  'https://one.tatvacare.in/api/resource/Error Log'
```

Every outbound HTTP call we make to a provider (WATI, Acefone) with its response. `status` = Completed/Failed; `url` = the endpoint (filter `url like "%wati.io%"` or `%acefone%`); `data`/`output` = request sent / raw provider response; `error` = failure detail.

post.summary: Create a task on a lead
post.description: Create a `CRM Task`. Setting `assigned_to` auto-creates the assignment ToDo and notifies the agent. If the linked lead's Product Line / Group / Program + `custom_task_type` matches a checklist template, the task is seeded with checklist rows and **cannot be set `Done`** until every required item is ticked.

custom_task_type.description: Link → CRM Task Type. Drives checklist seeding.

status.description: Backlog | Todo | In Progress | Done | Canceled

get.description: Standard REST list. Filter by `reference_docname` to get a lead's tasks.

post.description: Ensure ONE open task of `task_type` for a lead (throttle: one open task per lead per type). Returns the existing open task name if one exists, else creates it. This is the same method the inbound-WhatsApp event calls.

get.description: Read-only inventory of every live automation — code layer (doc_events, scheduler_events, override_*) and config layer (Assignment Rule, Server Script, Notification). System Manager only.

parameters.name: app
parameters.description: Filter to one app; omit for all.

post.description: Auto-assign leads to agents. One rule per Group (or Program), most-specific via `assign_condition`. No global default — an unmatched lead stays unowned. Fires on lead insert/update; the assignee is written to `lead_owner`. `name` is prompt-named — set it. System Manager only.

rule.description: Round Robin | Load Balancing | Based on Field | Weighted Distribution

assign_condition.description: Python expression evaluated on the document.

put.description: Disable (`{"disabled":1}`), edit users, or change the condition.

post.description: The type vocabulary referenced by `CRM Task.custom_task_type` and by checklist templates. `name = type_name`.

Defines the close-gate checklist for a `task_type`, scoped by optional Product Line / Group / Program (most-specific wins, no global default). A task gets these rows seeded when its lead + type match; required rows must be ticked before `Done`.

vertical.description: Optional — widen scope to a Product Line.

psp_group.description: Optional — widen scope to a Group.
